mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This signal is always active as it captures trend growth.
failure_condition: If over a 2-year rolling window, the monthly change in INDPRO shows no positive correlation with subsequent 20-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03), the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, pushing equity prices down over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If in instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp in 3 months, the forward 60-day SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Reserve keeps the funds rate at extremely low levels and the economy is growing, the combination of cheap credit and expanding activity amplifies equity valuations through compressed discount rates and higher earnings expectations, producing positive forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and GDPC1 annualized growth > 2% in the latest quarter
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with positive growth
failure_condition: If, when both conditions are met, the average 20-day SPX return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.05) over a 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, as the Federal Reserve raises the federal funds rate, discount rates increase and equity valuations contract, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased by a cumulative 25 basis points or more over the past 3 months
failure_condition: If the average forward 60-day SPX return in tightening cycles (as defined) is positive or not statistically below the unconditional mean (p<0.10) over any 5-year period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals a sudden drop in consumer spending, which rapidly feeds into lower earnings expectations and immediate negative equity market reaction over the following week.
failure_condition: If in weeks following a >=1% monthly decline in RSAFS, the 5-day SPX return is positive more often than negative, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously strong job growth and housing starts indicate a robust economic expansion with synchronized labor and housing demand, which supports corporate profits and equity prices over the coming quarter.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS year-over-year growth and HOUST year-over-year growth exceed 2%
failure_condition: If in periods when both conditions hold, the median forward 60-day SPX return is not positive, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase of the economic cycle, falling unemployment rates signal improving labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which drives positive equity returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by at least 0.2 percentage points over the past 3 months
regime_hint: early recovery phase (unemployment declining from cyclically high levels)
failure_condition: If in early recovery periods (UNRATE > 6% and declining), the average 20-day SPX return following a 0.2pp drop is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects a healthy expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter. This signal is continuously active as it captures the economic growth trend.
failure_condition: If the trailing 4-quarter GDP growth rate does not show a non-negative correlation with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over a 10-year backtest, the hypothesis is rejected.
